title: This monday.com account already belongs to a different slack workspace with the slug [Company name]
excerpt: Has anyone ever encountered this error when installing Monday.com to Slack? We are currently looking into changing messaging platform which is Slack. I am trying to test out its integration with Monday.com. However, this…

posts.author: owendelloson (Rowenna Delloson)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone ever encountered this error when installing Monday.com to Slack? We are currently looking into changing messaging platform which is Slack. I am trying to test out its integration with Monday.com. However, this error message appears and I don’t know what it means that we have another Monday.com account attached to a Slack workspace. Help, please! Thank you!

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
It sounds like you’re encountering a common issue with Monday.com and Slack integration. This error usually occurs when a Slack workspace is already connected to a different Monday.com account. Here’s how we can address this problem:

  1. Verify Existing Connections: Check if your Slack workspace is connected to another Monday.com account. You can do this by navigating to the Slack integration settings in Monday.com.
  2. Disconnect Unwanted Connections: If there’s another Monday.com account connected, you’ll need to disconnect it. This can be done by the account admin through the Monday.com integration settings.
  3. Re-Integrate: Once the unwanted connection is removed, try re-integrating your desired Monday.com account with Slack. Follow the prompts to authorize the new connection.
  4. Contact Support: If the issue persists, it might be best to contact Monday.com or Slack support for further assistance. They can help identify and resolve any underlying issues with the integration.

This should resolve the conflict and allow you to connect Monday.com with your Slack workspace successfully. If you need any further assistance or a walkthrough, feel free to reach out.
